ab: maqati aken a kivadaq tjanusun?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: maqati, kisalu aravc aken.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: maqati aken a kivadaq tjanusun?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: sikuda, inialiaw a ku kinljang.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: nu nakecengelj anga izua su zikang? saljinga ken tu vaik a kivala tjanusun a ki maqati?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: uii, ku kaljavau sun itjumaq.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: izua su zikang nutiyaw? sakivadaq aken tjanusun ta patjara tjaucikel nua se Payuan.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於排灣族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Paiwan tribe.

ab: uri vaik aken a sema vavua nu tiyaw, neka ken itjumaq.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: kemljang sun aravac tua kakudanan nua se Payuan, sa kitulu aken tjanusun.
ch: A:我聽說您對排灣族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Paiwan, so I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: ini a liyaw aravac aku kinljang, ljakua maqati aken a kipapuljat a temvela.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: maqati aken a pasasing? sikananguaqan a paqenetj aken nu vencik anga ken.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: uii, inikamakud.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: aki pida anga a su cavilj?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: izua 98 anga a ken.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: kakedriyan anan sun.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: ini lja, ramaljeng anga ken.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: kemasinu sun?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemasi Kuabar aken.
en: B: I’m from Kuabar.

ab: a su valjaw se Payuan uta?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是排灣族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also Paiwan?

ab: ini, se Piyumazuk a caucau timadju.
en: B: No, she is a Puyuma.

ab: anemanema nu kaiyan a su kinasaigu a qivu?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: saigu aken a qivu tua kai a nase Payuan kata kai nua se Piniyumayan.
ch: B: 我會說排灣語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak Paiwan and Puyuma.

ab: maleva aken a kivadaq tjanusun, pasemalavi amen, tua patjara sinanpalisi itua qemaljup?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: uii, ruqaljup aken kamaqacuvucuvung anan aken.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: masalu, bulai aravac a su tulu tucu a qadaw.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: paquliqulid angata a ku sinipasemalaw, tjaucikel a kemasisicuayan nua tja sevalitan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: patjemaza a ku kivadaq. masalu aravac tua su pasemalaw.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: inikamakuda, patjatjaucikeli anga nutjaivililj.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: ku vaikai anga, saljinga ken a lemangeda tua tjaliyaw a milimilingan nutjaivililj.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: uii lja, galju anga i djalan.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
